interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y (12.68%) peaks highest. The 2Y anomaly likely reflects tight banking sector liquidity and aggressive re-pricing around BB's 10% repo rate amid persistent inflation (9%+ range). The 364D dip below 182D suggests uneven demand at auctions. The 130bps 10Y-91D spread indicates moderate term premium, compressed by inflation expectations and AD/AS liquidity management challenges.
implications: The 2Y hump signals banks are demanding significant premium for medium-term lock-up amid liquidity stress and uncertain BB tightening trajectory
Inverted segments (364D < 182D, 2Y > 5Y) suggest fragmented demand and possible market expectation of eventual policy easing beyond 2 years
Steep long-end (15Y at 12.68%) reflects fiscal deficit concerns and inflation risk premium as government borrowing remains elevated
